pairs.question: Who is Dariusz Tomasz Michalczewski and what titles did he hold in boxing?
pairs.answer: Dariusz Tomasz Michalczewski, also known as Tiger, is a Polish boxer born on May 5, 1968. He held multiple prestigious titles in boxing, including the World Boxing Organization (WBO), World Boxing Association (WBA), International Boxing Federation (IBF), and Lineal championships in the light heavyweight division for nine years. He was undefeated for twelve years during his career.

pairs.question: When did Dariusz Tomasz Michalczewski start his professional boxing career and what was his first notable victory?
pairs.answer: Michalczewski began his professional boxing career in 1991. His first notable victory came when he won the World Boxing Association (WBA) title against Leeonzer Barber, who had been undefeated for three years prior to their fight.

pairs.question: How many weight classes did Dariusz Tomasz Michalczewski compete in during his career?
pairs.answer: Michalczewski competed in two weight classes during his career. He won the WBO title in the cruiserweight division against Nestor Hipolito Giovannini and held multiple titles in the light heavyweight division.

pairs.question: What was the outcome of the 1996 fight between Dariusz Tomasz Michalczewski and Graciano Rochigani?
pairs.answer: In 1996, Michalczewski fought Graciano Rochigani, one of the best light heavyweight boxers at the time. Michalczewski won the fight by knockout in the seventh round.

pairs.question: Explain the significance of the fight between Dariusz Tomasz Michalczewski and Virgil Hill in 1997.
pairs.answer: The fight against Virgil Hill in 1997 was significant because Michalczewski combined four major titles—WBO, WBA, IBF, and Lineal—into one by winning against Hill, who was considered one of the world's best boxers at the time.

pairs.question: What happened in the 2000 rematch between Dariusz Tomasz Michalczewski and Graciano Rochigani?
pairs.answer: In the 2000 rematch, Michalczewski won against Graciano Rochigani by knockout in the tenth round. This victory was notable as it marked the end of the fight in the later stages, showcasing Michalczewski's endurance and skill.

pairs.question: Why did Dariusz Tomasz Michalczewski fail to break Rocky Marciano's undefeated record in 2003?
pairs.answer: Michalczewski failed to break Rocky Marciano's 49-fight undefeated record in his 49th fight. In this fight, held in Hamburg against Julio Cesar Gonzalez, he was unprepared and lost by split decision, ending his chance to tie Marciano's record.

pairs.question: What was the outcome of Dariusz Tomasz Michalczewski's comeback fight in 2005 against Fabrice Tiozzo?
pairs.answer: In 2005, Michalczewski made a comeback but lost by knockout in the fifth round to Fabrice Tiozzo, who was the WBA champion at the time. After this fight, Michalczewski announced that he would never fight again.
